summary: Backend-only testing of the newly added /api/health keepalive endpoint plus related read-only endpoints (root, /api/coins, /api/session/status). 16/16 pytest tests pass. The /api/health endpoint returns HTTP 200 in ~90-150ms externally with the correct shape (status='alive', ISO timestamp, integer websocket_clients, bool session_active). It is DB-free and safe for cron-job.org to ping every 10 minutes to keep the Render free tier awake.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~314 lines: still manageable, but the WebSocket broadcast logic + background scanner startup could be moved into services/ for cleaner separation as the file grows.
broadcast_signal / broadcast_candle share the same disconnect-cleanup pattern -- consider extracting a single _broadcast(message) helper (DRY).

websocket_clients is a module-level list mutated from multiple async tasks; on a single Uvicorn worker this is safe, but if scaled to multiple workers on Render it will silently only broadcast to clients on the same worker. Worth documenting.

The external Kubernetes ingress routes '/' to the React frontend, so the backend root endpoint ('/') is only reachable via port 8001 directly. On Render this is not an issue (Render exposes the FastAPI service directly), so no action needed here -- just noted for awareness. cron-job.org should ping /api/health, not /.
/api/health does NOT touch MongoDB or the Bitunix WebSocket -- confirmed lightweight (avg ~90ms external latency). Good design choice for a keepalive endpoint.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Test file /app/backend/tests/test_health_endpoints.py covers: (1) /api/health -- status 200, JSON shape, status='alive', ISO timestamp <60s old, websocket_clients int, session_active bool, response <3s; (2) backend root '/' -- tested via http://localhost:8001 because K8s ingress serves React app at '/' externally; (3) /api/coins -- 10 coins, contains BTCUSDT/ETHUSDT/SOLUSDT; (4) /api/session/status -- is_active bool, ISO current_time_utc, sessions dict. External URL used: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env. Measured external latency of /api/health: ~90-150ms across 3 requests.
rca of the issue: No issues found. The initial test for root '/' via external URL failed because the K8s ingress in the preview environment routes non-/api/* paths to the frontend; fixed by testing root via localhost:8001 which is where the FastAPI '/' endpoint actually lives. This is a preview-env artifact, not a code bug -- on Render.com the FastAPI '/' will be directly exposed.
